{{参照方法|date=2019年9月11日 (水) 15:08 (UTC)}}
'''Nukata no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (''nukata no kuni no miyatsuko'', ''nukata kokuzō'') was a {{ill|kuni no miyatsuko|ja|国造}} who ruled the western part of {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}} (or the eastern part of {{ill|Ōmi Province|ja|近江国}}).

== Overview ==
=== Name ===
In the "Kokuzō Hongi" of the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' it is written '''Nukata no Kuni no Miyatsuko'''.

=== Ancestry ===
According to the ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' "Kokuzō Hongi", '''{{ill|Ōmorokо-no-Mikoto|ja|大直侶宇命|lt=Ōmoroko-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=大真侶古命}}''', grandson of '''{{ill|Hikokunibuku-no-Mikoto|ja|彦国葺命}}''' — ancestor of the {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和邇氏}} — was appointed Nukata no Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}.

=== Clan ===
'''{{ill|Nukata clan|ja|額田氏}}''' (''nukata-uji''; {{ill|kabane|ja|カバネ|lt=kabane|lt_ja=姓}} of {{ill|Atai (kabane)|ja|直 (姓)|lt=Atai|lt_ja=直}}). Some later members were granted the {{ill|Sukune|ja|宿禰}} kabane. Of the same lineage as the {{ill|Wani clan|ja|和珥氏}}, {{ill|Awata clan|ja|粟田氏}}, {{ill|Mano clan|ja|真野氏}}, {{ill|Muza no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|武社国造}}, {{ill|Kibi-Anakuni no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|吉備穴国造}}, and others.

== Base ==
The kuni no miyatsuko's base was Nukata-gō in {{ill|Ikeda District|ja|池田郡}} of Mino Province, or {{ill|Sakata District|ja|坂田郡}} of Ōmi Province — corresponding to present-day {{ill|Ikeda, Gifu|ja|池田町 (岐阜県)|lt=Ikeda|lt_ja=池田町}} in {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}} or {{ill|Maibara, Shiga|ja|米原市|lt=Maibara|lt_ja=米原市}} in {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}.

=== Territory ===
The kuni no miyatsuko's territory was the region then called '''Nukata Province''', corresponding to parts of the later {{ill|Ikeda District|ja|池田郡}}, {{ill|Ōno District, Mino|ja|大野郡 (美濃国)|lt=Ōno District|lt_ja=大野郡}}, and {{ill|Anpachi District|ja|安八郡}}.

== Tutelary Shrines ==
'''{{ill|Hananaga-kami Shrine|ja|花長上神社}}''' and '''{{ill|Hananaga-shimo Shrine|ja|花長下神社}}'''.

=== Tombs ===
*'''{{ill|Kamiiso Kofun Cluster|ja|上磯古墳群}}''' (''kamiiso kofungun'')
*: A kofun cluster built from the latter half of the {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} to the first half of the {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}}, consisting of 2 {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun (rectangle)|ja|前方後方墳}} and 1 {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}}.
*'''{{ill|No Kofun Cluster|ja|野古墳群}}''' (''no kofungun'')
*: A kofun cluster built from the middle of the 5th century to the beginning of the {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, consisting of 8 keyhole-shaped kofun and numerous round kofun.

== Descendants ==
*{{ill|Nukata-no-Imatari|ja|額田今足}} (''nukata-no-imatari'')
*: A {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} jurist and Myōbō-hakase. He petitioned for the official codification of commentary on the ritsuryō and was a forerunner of the compilation of the ''{{ill|Ryō-no-gige|ja|令義解}}''.

== References ==
{{No footnotes|section=1|date=2019年9月11日 (水) 15:08 (UTC)}}
{{ページ番号|section=1|date=2019年9月11日 (水) 15:08 (UTC)}}
*''Kokushi Daijiten'' ({{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}})
* Sakamoto Tarō, Hirano Kunio, ''Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan)
*''Nihon Kodai Jinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan)
*''Nihon-shi Kō-jiten'' ({{ill|Yamakawa Shuppansha|ja|山川出版社}})
*''Shintō Daijiten'' (Rinsen Shoten)
*''Nihon-shi Sōran'' ({{ill|Shinjinbutsu Ōraisha|ja|新人物往来社}})

== See also ==
*{{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}

{{Japanese-history-stub}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:ぬかたのくにのみやつこ}}
[[Category:額田氏| ]]
[[Category:和珥氏|*]]
[[Category:美濃国]]
[[Category:近江国]]
[[Category:岐阜県の歴史]]
[[Category:滋賀県の歴史]]
[[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]]
[[ja:額田国造]]
